2026-04-23T15:30:08.7994592Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T15:30:08.7995192Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T15:30:08.7995793Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T15:30:08.7996181Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T15:30:08.7996500Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T15:30:08.7996734Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T15:30:08.7996888Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-23T15:30:08.7997454Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-23T15:30:08.7998202Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

The CHANGELOG now records the `1.0.0` stable release, but the README still lacks concrete code examples and meaningful Key concepts content suitable for a GA library.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `tests/Samples/Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs` contain full working code examples but use no `#region Snippet:` tags, so `Update-Snippets.ps1` cannot inject them into the README\\n- **Key concepts section is generic** — it only links to generic Azure SDK docs and does not explain service-specific concepts such as `DevOpsPoolResource`, `DevOpsVmssFabricProfile`, `DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile`, or `DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile`\\n- **Authenticate the client section has no code snippet** — it only links to a quickstart guide rather than showing a short `ArmClient` + `DefaultAzureCredential` instantiation example\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/README.md` — Edit\\n\\n1. **Authenticate the client** — replace the single-sentence link with a short snippet placeholder after the existing text:\\n\\n```markdown\\nTo create an authenticated client and start interacting with Microsoft Azure resources, please install the [`Azure.Identity`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md) package:\\n\\n```\\ndotnet add package Azure.Identity\\n```\\n\\nThen instantiate an `ArmClient`:\\n\\n```\\nTokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\nArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n2. **Key concepts** — replace the single generic link with a section that describes the main types:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n### DevOpsPoolResource\\nRepresents a Managed DevOps Pool — a pool of Azure virtual machines used as Azure DevOps agents. Each pool is tied to a resource group and an Azure DevOps organization.\\n\\n### DevOpsVmssFabricProfile\\nDescribes the underlying Azure VMSS-based infrastructure for the pool, including VM SKU, OS images, network settings, and OS secrets management.\\n\\n### DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile\\nAssociates the pool with one or more Azure DevOps organizations and projects.\\n\\n### DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile / DevOpsStatefulAgentProfile\\nConfigures whether agents are stateless (fresh VM per job) or stateful (VM reused across jobs).\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n3. **Examples** — replace the generic link with snippet placeholders:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a Managed DevOps Pool\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Get a Managed DevOps Pool\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List all Managed DevOps Pools in a resource group\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Delete a Managed DevOps Pool\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nAdd `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` tags to the existing sample files so `Update-Snippets.ps1` can inject code into the README placeholders.\\n\\n#### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs`\\n\\nIn the `CreateOrUpdate_PoolsCreateOrUpdate` method body, wrap the meaningful portion (after the client/resource group boilerplate) with:\\n\\n```csharp\\n#region Snippet:CreateDevOpsPool\\n// ... (pool creation code) ...\\n#endregion\\n```\\n\\nAdd a new minimal `Get_PoolsGet` method annotated with `[Test]` and `[Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\n[Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\npublic async Task Get_PoolsGet()\\n{\\n    // get your azure access token\\n    TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n    ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n    string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;a2e95d27-c161-4b61-bda4-11512c14c2c2\\u0026#34;;\\n    string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;rg\\u0026#34;;\\n    ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n    ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\\n    DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools();\\n\\n    #region Snippet:GetDevOpsPool\\n    string poolName = \\u0026#34;pool\\u0026#34;;\\n    DevOpsPoolResource result = await collection.GetAsync(poolName);\\n    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Pool name: {result.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n    #endregion\\n}\\n```\\n\\nAdd a new `List_PoolsList` method:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\n[Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\npublic async Task List_PoolsList()\\n{\\n    TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n    ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n    string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;a2e95d27-c161-4b61-bda4-11512c14c2c2\\u0026#34;;\\n    string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;rg\\u0026#34;;\\n    ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n    ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\\n    DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools();\\n\\n    #region Snippet:ListDevOpsPools\\n    await foreach (DevOpsPoolResource pool in collection.GetAllAsync())\\n    {\\n        Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Pool: {pool.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n    }\\n    #endregion\\n}\\n```\\n\\n#### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs`\\n\\nFind the delete method and wrap the relevant lines:\\n\\n```csharp\\n#region Snippet:DeleteDevOpsPool\\nArmOperation lro = await pool.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\\n#endregion\\n```\\n\\n#### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_ResourceGroupResourceExtensions.cs`\\n\\nAdd a `#region Snippet:CreateDevOpsInfrastructureClient` / `#endregion` block around the `ArmClient` instantiation lines.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/README.md` already contains all required sections, but the following need content added or improved:\\n\\n| Section | Status | Action needed |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started → Authenticate the client | ⚠️ Link only | Add code snippet placeholder |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic link only | Add service-specific type descriptions |\\n| Examples | ❌ No code | Add snippet placeholders for create/get/list/delete |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present | No change |\\n\\nUse wired to the README** — `tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs`, `tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs`, `tests/Samples/Sample_ResourceGroupResourceExtensions.cs`, and `tests/Samples/Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs` contain full working code examples but use no `#region Snippet:` tags, so `Update-Snippets.ps1` cannot inject them into the README\\n- **Key concepts section is generic** — it only links to generic Azure SDK docs and does not explain service-specific concepts such as `DevOpsPoolResource`, `DevOpsVmssFabricProfile`, `DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile`, or `DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile`\\n- **Authenticate the client section has no code snippet** — it only links to a quickstart guide rather than showing a short `ArmClient` + `DefaultAzureCredential` instantiation example\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/README.md` — Edit\\n\\n1. **Authenticate the client** — replace the single-sentence link with a short snippet placeholder after the existing text:\\n\\n```markdown\\nTo create an authenticated client and start interacting with Microsoft Azure resources, please install the [`Azure.Identity`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md) package:\\n\\n```\\ndotnet add package Azure.Identity\\n```\\n\\nThen instantiate an `ArmClient`:\\n\\n```\\nTokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\nArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n2. **Key concepts** — replace the single generic link with a section that describes the main types:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n### DevOpsPoolResource\\nRepresents a Managed DevOps Pool — a pool of Azure virtual machines used as Azure DevOps agents. Each pool is tied to a resource group and an Azure DevOps organization.\\n\\n### DevOpsVmssFabricProfile\\nDescribes the underlying Azure VMSS-based infrastructure for the pool, including VM SKU, OS images, network settings, and OS secrets management.\\n\\n### DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile\\nAssociates the pool with one or more Azure DevOps organizations and projects.\\n\\n### DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile / DevOpsStatefulAgentProfile\\nConfigures whether agents are stateless (fresh VM per job) or stateful (VM reused across jobs).\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n3. **Examples** — replace the generic link with snippet placeholders:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a Managed DevOps Pool\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Get a Managed DevOps Pool\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List all Managed DevOps Pools in a resource group\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Delete a Managed DevOps Pool\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nAdd `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` tags to the existing sample files so `Update-Snippets.ps1` can inject code into the README placeholders.\\n\\n#### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs`\\n\\nIn the `CreateOrUpdate_PoolsCreateOrUpdate` method body, wrap the meaningful portion (after the client/resource group boilerplate) with:\\n\\n```csharp\\n#region Snippet:CreateDevOpsPool\\n// ... (pool creation code) ...\\n#endregion\\n```\\n\\nAdd a new minimal `Get_PoolsGet` method annotated with `[Test]` and `[Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\n[Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\npublic async Task Get_PoolsGet()\\n{\\n    // get your azure access token\\n    TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n    ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n    string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;a2e95d27-c161-4b61-bda4-11512c14c2c2\\u0026#34;;\\n    string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;rg\\u0026#34;;\\n    ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n    ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\\n    DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools();\\n\\n    #region Snippet:GetDevOpsPool\\n    string poolName = \\u0026#34;pool\\u0026#34;;\\n    DevOpsPoolResource result = await collection.GetAsync(poolName);\\n    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Pool name: {result.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n    #endregion\\n}\\n```\\n\\nAdd a new `List_PoolsList` method:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\n[Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\npublic async Task List_PoolsList()\\n{\\n    TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n    ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n    string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;a2e95d27-c161-4b61-bda4-11512c14c2c2\\u0026#34;;\\n    string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;rg\\u0026#34;;\\n    ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n    ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\\n    DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools();\\n\\n    #region Snippet:ListDevOpsPools\\n    await foreach (DevOpsPoolResource pool in collection.GetAllAsync())\\n    {\\n        Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Pool: {pool.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n    }\\n    #endregion\\n}\\n```\\n\\n#### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs`\\n\\nFind the delete method and wrap the relevant lines:\\n\\n```csharp\\n#region Snippet:DeleteDevOpsPool\\nArmOperation lro = await pool.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\\n#endregion\\n```\\n\\n#### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_ResourceGroupResourceExtensions.cs`\\n\\nAdd a `#region Snippet:CreateDevOpsInfrastructureClient` / `#endregion` block around the `ArmClient` instantiation lines.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/README.md` already contains all required sections, but the following need content added or improved:\\n\\n| Section | Status | Action needed |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started → Authenticate the client | ⚠️ Link only | Add code snippet placeholder |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic link only | Add service-specific type descriptions |\\n| Examples | ❌ No code | Add snippet placeholders for create/get/list/delete |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present | No change |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Verify the git diff shows README updated with code.\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 devopsinfrastructure` — only needed if public types or members changed (not required for docs-only changes)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/` — ensures consistent code formatting\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
